message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="luca-palmieri" color="blue" summary="Waiting For implementation ready for review">
The Waiting For feature implementation (Step 8 / US-GTD-5) is complete and all pipeline checks pass. Here is a summary for review:

## Implementation Summary

### Domain Layer (`src/domain/waiting_for_item.rs`)
- `WaitingForId(Uuid)` — newtype wrapper
- `WaitingOnPerson` — non-empty, max 100 chars, trimmed; constructed via `WaitingOnPerson::parse()`
- `WaitingForItem` enum — `Active` / `Completed` variants (same state machine pattern as NextAction/Project)
- 15 unit tests covering construction, validation, state transitions

### Infrastructure Layer (`src/infrastructure/waiting_for_repository.rs`)
- `insert_waiting_for_item`, `find_active_waiting_for_items_by_user`, `find_waiting_for_item_by_id`
- `complete_waiting_for_item`, `delete_waiting_for_item`, `count_active_waiting_for_items`, `update_waiting_for_item`
- Migration: `waiting_for_items` table with id, user_id (FK), title, waiting_on, created_at, completed_at

### Service Layer (`src/services/waiting_for_service.rs`)
- `add_waiting_for_item`, `list_active_waiting_for_items`, `count_active_waiting_for_items`
- `complete_waiting_for_item`, `delete_waiting_for_item`, `update_waiting_for_item`
- Error types: `AddWaitingForError`, `CompleteWaitingForError`, `DeleteWaitingForError`, `UpdateWaitingForError`
- All service functions have tracing instrumentation

### Routes (`src/routes/waiting_for.rs`)
- GET /waiting-for — list active items
- POST /waiting-for — add new item (title + waiting_on person)
- POST /waiting-for/{id}/complete — mark as received
- POST /waiting-for/{id}/delete — delete item
- GET /waiting-for/{id}/edit — get inline edit form (HTMX)
- POST /waiting-for/{id}/edit — update item
- GET /waiting-for/{id} — get single item fragment (for cancel edit)
- Full HTMX support with HX-Trigger announcements

### Templates
- `waiting_for.html` — main page with two-field add form ("What are you waiting for?" + "Who or what?"), empty state, semantic list
- `waiting_for_item.html` — item partial with Edit link, Received button, Delete button, all with aria-labels
- `waiting_for_edit.html` — inline edit form with title + waiting_on inputs, Save/Cancel, visually hidden labels

### Dashboard Integration
- Dynamic waiting_for_count displayed on dashboard stat card

### CSS (`static/css/main.css`)
- ~150 lines of waiting-for component CSS in @layer components
- Follows existing patterns (similar to projects, next-actions)

### Focus Management JS (`static/js/waiting-for-focus.js`)
- WCAG 2.4.3 compliant focus management for HTMX swaps
- Handles delete/complete (focus next/prev sibling), edit mode, empty state transitions

### Tests
- 85 unit tests (15 new for WaitingForItem domain)
- 220 integration tests (30 new for waiting-for routes)
- 11 Playwright e2e tests (all pass, no regression)

### Glossary (`docs/glossary.md`)
- Updated with WaitingForItem, WaitingForId, WaitingOnPerson types
- Added waiting-for actions, error types, state machine diagram, error copy convention

### Pipeline Status
- `cargo fmt --check`: PASS
- `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`: PASS
- `cargo test`: PASS (305 total)
- `npx playwright test`: PASS (11 e2e)

Please review and provide feedback. I am ready to commit once we have consensus.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Thorough review complete. Let me send my feedback.

input.content: Lea here with my full CSS/frontend review of the Waiting For implementation. ## Overall Assessment The CSS is well-structured, follows design tokens consistently, uses logical properties throughout, and stays within `@layer components`. BEM naming is consistent with our existing components. The templates use semantic HTML with proper accessibility attributes. Focus management JS follows the established pattern from other pages. Good work. ## BLOCKING Issues **1. Missing `` element for date display** (`waiting_for_item.html:6`) Currently: ```html {{ item.date_added }} ``` Should use semantic `` element: ```html {{ item.date_added }} ``` The `` element is semantic HTML that helps screen readers and machines parse dates. This requires the template struct to provide both a machine-readable ISO datetime string and a human-readable display string. The US-GTD-5 acceptance criteria says "Each item shows: title, who/what it's waiting on, **date added**" — the `` element ensures this is properly conveyed. Every other date-displaying page in the web standards world uses `` for this purpose. **2. Empty state text does not match user story** (`waiting_for.html:59`) Currently: ``` Nothing to follow up on. Items you're waiting on from others will appear here. ``` US-GTD-5 specifies: ``` Nothing pending from others. When you delegate something or need to follow up, add it here. ``` I flagged this in my design review feedback. The user story text includes an actionable instruction ("add it here") which is better UX. Please use the specified copy. **3. Missing `waiting_on` pill-badge styling** (`waiting_for_item.html:4`, `main.css:2230-2233`) The `waiting_on` person/entity text is currently displayed as plain muted text: ```css .waiting-for-item__person { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--color-fg-muted); } ``` This should use the pill-badge pattern consistent with how we display context tags (`.next-action-item__context`) to visually distinguish the metadata from the title: ```css .waiting-for-item__person { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--color-fg-muted); padding: var(--space-xs) var(--space-sm); background-color: var(--color-gray-100); border-radius: var(--radius-full); } ``` Without this, the person text visually blends into the title, making it harder to scan the list quickly. Visual distinction of metadata is important for the "see everything they are waiting on in one glance" success metric. ## Minor Issues **4. Add form layout differs from established pattern** (`main.css:2164-2196`) The add form uses a stacked vertical layout (`flex-direction: column`) with two separate groups, while every other add form in the app (inbox, next-actions, projects) uses the horizontal `flex-wrap: wrap` pattern with `align-items: end`. This inconsistency is noticeable when navigating between pages. I understand the two-field form motivated this choice (and the vertical stack does work), so I won't block on it — but consider whether aligning with the established horizontal row pattern would be worth it for visual consistency. Not blocking. **5. `color: var(--color-fg-subtle)` on date** (`main.css:2238`) `--color-fg-subtle` maps to `gray-400` which is 3:1 contrast. Per our token documentation, this is "NOT for body text" — it's acceptable only for large text or UI components. The date text at `font-size-xs` is small text, so 3:1 may not meet AA for that size. Consider using `var(--color-fg-muted)` (gray-600, ~7:1) instead, which comfortably passes AA at any text size. Not blocking since the text is supplementary, but worth fixing. ## Nice-to-have **6. Edit form labels are visually hidden** (`waiting_for_edit.html:11,21`) The edit form uses `class="visually-hidden"` on both labels ("Title" and "Who or what"). This is fine for screen readers, but since there are two input fields stacked vertically, sighted users might not know which field is which (the placeholders help, but placeholders disappear on focus). Consider making at least the "Who or what" label visible since the two fields look identical otherwise. Not blocking — the placeholders are adequate for now. ## Summary - **3 BLOCKING**: `` element, empty state copy, person pill-badge styling - **2 Minor**: add form layout consistency, date contrast - **1 Nice-to-have**: visible edit form labels Everything else looks good — the CSS architecture, token usage, logical properties, focus management JS, dashboard integration, HTMX patterns, and accessibility attributes are all solid.

BEM naming is consistent with our existing components. The templates use semantic HTML with proper accessibility attributes. Focus management JS follows the established pattern from other pages. Good work. ## BLOCKING Issues **1. Missing `` element for date display** (`waiting_for_item.html:6`) Currently: ```html {{ item.date_added }} ``` Should use semantic `` element: ```html {{ item.date_added }} ``` The `` element is semantic HTML that helps screen readers and machines parse dates. This requires the template struct to provide both a machine-readable ISO datetime string and a human-readable display string. The US-GTD-5 acceptance criteria says "Each item shows: title, who/what it's waiting on, **date added**" — the `` element ensures this is properly conveyed. Every other date-displaying page in the web standards world uses `` for this purpose. **2. Empty state text does not match user story** (`waiting_for.html:59`) Currently: ``` Nothing to follow up on. Items you're waiting on from others will appear here. ``` US-GTD-5 specifies: ``` Nothing pending from others. When you delegate something or need to follow up, add it here. ``` I flagged this in my design review feedback. The user story text includes an actionable instruction ("add it here") which is better UX. Please use the specified copy. **3. Missing `waiting_on` pill-badge styling** (`waiting_for_item.html:4`, `main.css:2230-2233`) The `waiting_on` person/entity text is currently displayed as plain muted text: ```css .waiting-for-item__person { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--color-fg-muted); } ``` This should use the pill-badge pattern consistent with how we display context tags (`.next-action-item__context`) to visually distinguish the metadata from the title: ```css .waiting-for-item__person { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--color-fg-muted); padding: var(--space-xs) var(--space-sm); background-color: var(--color-gray-100); border-radius: var(--radius-full); } ``` Without this, the person text visually blends into the title, making it harder to scan the list quickly. Visual distinction of metadata is important for the "see everything they are waiting on in one glance" success metric. ## Minor Issues **4. Add form layout differs from established pattern** (`main.css:2164-2196`) The add form uses a stacked vertical layout (`flex-direction: column`) with two separate groups, while every other add form in the app (inbox, next-actions, projects) uses the horizontal `flex-wrap: wrap` pattern with `align-items: end`. This inconsistency is noticeable when navigating between pages. I understand the two-field form motivated this choice (and the vertical stack does work), so I won't block on it — but consider whether aligning with the established horizontal row pattern would be worth it for visual consistency. Not blocking. **5. `color: var(--color-fg-subtle)` on date** (`main.css:2238`) `--color-fg-subtle` maps to `gray-400` which is 3:1 contrast. Per our token documentation, this is "NOT for body text" — it's acceptable only for large text or UI components. The date text at `font-size-xs` is small text, so 3:1 may not meet AA for that size. Consider using `var(--color-fg-muted)` (gray-600, ~7:1) instead, which comfortably passes AA at any text size. Not blocking since the text is supplementary, but worth fixing. ## Nice-to-have **6. Edit form labels are visually hidden** (`waiting_for_edit.html:11,21`) The edit form uses `class="visually-hidden"` on both labels ("Title" and "Who or what"). This is fine for screen readers, but since there are two input fields stacked vertically, sighted users might not know which field is which (the placeholders help, but placeholders disappear on focus). Consider making at least the "Who or what" label visible since the two fields look identical otherwise. Not blocking — the placeholders are adequate for now. ## Summary - **3 BLOCKING**: `` element, empty state copy, person pill-badge styling - **2 Minor**: add form layout consistency, date contrast - **1 Nice-to-have**: visible edit form labels Everything else looks good — the CSS architecture, token usage, logical properties, focus management JS, dashboard integration, HTMX patterns, and accessibility attributes are all solid." } }

content.text:
I've completed my review. Here's what I found:

**3 BLOCKING issues:**
1. Missing `<time>` element for date display in `waiting_for_item.html` -- semantic HTML requirement
2. Empty state text doesn't match the user story specification (actionable copy is better UX)
3. `waiting_on` person text needs pill-badge styling for visual scannability (currently plain muted text, should match our context badge pattern)

**2 Minor issues:**
4. Add form uses a vertical stack layout instead of our established horizontal flex-wrap pattern
5. Date text uses `--color-fg-subtle` (3:1 contrast) at `font-size-xs` which may not meet AA

**1 Nice-to-have:**
6. Both edit form labels are visually hidden -- with two inputs stacked, visible labels would help sighted users distinguish the fields

Overall the implementation is solid. CSS architecture, design tokens, logical properties, BEM naming, focus management JS, HTMX patterns, and accessibility attributes are all well done. Once the 3 blocking items are addressed, I'll approve.
